quick_skim.title: Lighting-Aware Representation Learning under Controllable Lighting Variation
quick_skim.abstract: Variations in illumination remain a major challenge for visual representation learning, as they induce substantial appearance changes both across and within environments. While existing approaches typically address this issue through data augmentations that encourage models to become invariant to lighting changes, such strategies do not explicitly model lighting information during learning. Inspired by theories of human vision, we propose a lighting-aware representation learning framework that incorporates illumination variation as an explicit training signal rather than a nuisance factor to be suppressed. Our method extends contrastive learning by introducing an auxiliary objective that captures illumination-dependent variation in rendered scenes, enabling the model to jointly learn representations that preserve semantic consistency while remaining sensitive to lighting-dependent visual structure. We evaluate the proposed model on image classification and object detection tasks across the ImageNet, ExDark, and PASCAL VOC benchmarks. Results demonstrate that the proposed lighting-aware training consistently improves downstream performance over standard contrastive learning baselines, while maintaining the same architecture and training budget. Furthermore, our approach shows promising performance in supervised learning frameworks and under settings involving simpler lighting variation, suggesting broad applicability beyond complex illumination scenarios. These results indicate its potential to enhance model robustness and adaptability in complex visual environments as well as in more conventional image processing tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Lighting-aware representation learning for visual tasks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 光照感知表示学习用于视觉任务
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 光照感知表示学习用于视觉任务
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 光照感知表示学习用于视觉任务
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes lighting-aware representation learning that explicitly models illumination variation to improve visual representations. Method could be transferable to fundus image classification where lighting conditions vary.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一种光照感知表示学习框架，将光照变化作为显式训练信号而非干扰因素，通过对比学习中的辅助目标捕捉光照依赖的变化。该方法可用于眼底图像分类，因为眼底成像常受光照条件影响，有助于提升模型对光照变化的鲁棒性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出一种光照感知表示学习框架，将光照变化作为显式训练信号而非干扰因素，通过对比学习中的辅助目标捕捉光照依赖的变化。该方法可用于眼底图像分类，因为眼底成像常受光照条件影响，有助于提升模型对光照变化的鲁棒性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: deep learning methods for fundus image classification

quick_skim.title: AUCp: Pseudo-AUC for Inference Model Selection with Unlabeled Validation Data in Abnormality Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Abnormality detection is a crucial yet challenging task in medical image analysis. Distinguishing abnormalities from normal data by learning to reconstruct normal-only data alleviates the reliance on labeled datasets. However, many studies, even if unsupervised, rely on a labeled validation set to select the best model for inference from multiple training iterations. For many diseases labeled data are unavailable and substantially time consuming to obtain. To address this, AUCp - a novel metric that supports abnormality detection for unsupervised and self-supervised methods is proposed. Instead of evaluating the realism of reconstructed images to select the best of model for inference, it focuses on actual detection performance and without requiring an annotated test set. Assuming the pseudo ground truth of all unannotated samples in the test set as abnormal/positive and using traditional AUC calculation, AUCp scores are derived. Given a large and representative training set of normal samples, we show mathematical and empirical evidence that model selection using AUCp scores improves disease detection in terms of unsupervised and self-supervised methods over conventional metrics. Using two unsupervised methods for neurologic disease detection and self-supervised methods on diverse datasets, our results demonstrate that the AUCp score effectively identifies the optimal model for inference, significantly enhancing abnormality and disease detection. The corresponding implementations are available in https://github.com/mahfuzmohammad/AUCp.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes a metric for unsupervised abnormality detection applicable to glaucoma detection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出一种用于无监督异常检测的指标，可用于青光眼检测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出一种用于无监督异常检测的指标，可用于青光眼检测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出一种用于无监督异常检测的指标，可用于青光眼检测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AUCp is a novel metric for selecting the best model for abnormality detection without labeled validation data, using pseudo-AUC, which could improve glaucoma detection from fundus images.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对异常检测中无标签验证数据难以选择最佳模型的问题，本文提出AUCp指标，基于伪AUC进行模型选择，无需人工标注。该方法专注于实际检测性能而非重建逼真度，可提升眼底图像中青光眼等病变的检测效果。与现有方法相比，AUCp在多个医学影像数据集上展现出优越的模型选择能力，为无监督病变检测提供可靠工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对异常检测中无标签验证数据难以选择最佳模型的问题，本文提出AUCp指标，基于伪AUC进行模型选择，无需人工标注。该方法专注于实际检测性能而非重建逼真度，可提升眼底图像中青光眼等病变的检测效果。与现有方法相比，AUCp在多个医学影像数据集上展现出优越的模型选择能力，为无监督病变检测提供可靠工具。

quick_skim.title: Untrained Convolutional Neural Networks as Feature Extractors for Structural MRI
quick_skim.abstract: We demonstrate that features extracted from structural MRI using un-CNN, an untrained convolutional neural network, achieve predictive performance comparable to or exceeding that of state-of-the-art pretrained foundation models across three structural MRI datasets and three downstream tasks. Un-CNN extends a classical 3D CNN architecture with multi-channel inputs, a hierarchical encoder with multi-scale feature aggregation, and covariance pooling. Untrained CNNs circumvent several key limitations of trained models, including high computational cost and memory requirements, the need to distribute large model weights, risks of data leakage, and challenges in reproducibility.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: untrained CNN as feature extractor that could be used for fundus image classification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 未训练CNN作为特征提取器，可用于眼底图像分类
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 未训练CNN作为特征提取器，可用于眼底图像分类
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 未训练CNN作为特征提取器，可用于眼底图像分类
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Untrained CNN (Un-CNN) achieves competitive performance as a feature extractor for structural MRI, suggesting it could be a lightweight alternative for fundus image classification without pretraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对预训练模型计算成本高、数据泄露风险等问题，本文提出未训练CNN（Un-CNN）作为特征提取器，在结构性MRI上取得与预训练基础模型相当或更好的预测性能。Un-CNN采用多通道输入、分层编码器与多尺度特征聚合、协方差池化，无需训练权重即可提取有效特征。该方法可推广到眼底图像分类任务，提供轻量级且可复现的深度学习方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对预训练模型计算成本高、数据泄露风险等问题，本文提出未训练CNN（Un-CNN）作为特征提取器，在结构性MRI上取得与预训练基础模型相当或更好的预测性能。Un-CNN采用多通道输入、分层编码器与多尺度特征聚合、协方差池化，无需训练权重即可提取有效特征。该方法可推广到眼底图像分类任务，提供轻量级且可复现的深度学习方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: deep learning methods for fundus image classification
